title: primula farinosa var. pinnata
intro: Primula farinosa var. pinnata, also known as the meadow cowslip or white cowslip, is a rare and unique variety of the primrose family, characterized by its white or yellowish flowers and pinnate leaves. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties, making it useful in treating urinary tract infections, fever, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Primula farinosa var. pinnata include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system, while its herbal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and salves. In traditional folk medicine, the plant is often used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant properties.
